primary_concern: Sepsis with multi-organ dysfunction and acute kidney injury (AKI Stage 3)
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) and WBC (14.2 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis. Progressive AKI (Stage 1 to Stage 3) with creatinine 8.9 mg/dL indicates severe renal impairment. Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring. Hyperbilirubinemia (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL) suggest hepatic and hematologic involvement. No evidence of septic shock due to unconfirmed vasopressor requirement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and elevated creatinine meet Sepsis-3 criteria
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: AKI Stage 3 confirmed by creatinine 8.9 mg/dL (≥3x baseline)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline in 7d

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis protocol with broad-spectrum antibiotics
Assess for vasopressor requirement to rule out septic shock
Order urgent infectious workup (blood cultures, imaging)
Evaluate for renal replacement therapy given AKI Stage 3
Consider platelet transfusion if <50 K/uL or active bleeding
doctor_handoff: Review for source control of infection, confirm vasopressor dependency, and assess need for ICU admission. Monitor for progression to multi-organ failure.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
Lactate 4.2 mmol/L, platelets 88-95 K/uL, AKI Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL)
AKI Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) with persistent thrombocytopenia

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
